items.title: China-Linked Fake Consulting Sites Targeting US Clearance Holders Seized
items.description: US Federal authorities have seized 13 domains allegedly used in a Chinese intelligence-linked operation to recruit Americans with access to classified government information. The websites posed as legitimate consulting firms, offering vague consultancy and advisory roles to current and former US government employees, military personnel, and security clearance holders. The operation, which began in
items.ai_summary: The seized domains were used in a social engineering campaign targeting US clearance holders, posing as legitimate consulting firms to recruit Americans with access to classified government information. The attack vector involved creating fake job postings and consultancy roles to lure victims into divulging sensitive information. If exploited, victims may have unknowingly provided their security clearance information, potentially compromising national security. To remediate, defenders should educate users on the risks of social engineering and implement robust background checks on job postings. Additionally, users should verify the authenticity of job postings and consultancy firms before engaging with them.
items.workflow_graph:
graph LR
    A([Threat Actor]):::actor -->|T1566| B[Initial Access]:::tactic
    B -->|T1059.001| C[Execution]:::tactic
    C -->|T1041| D[Command and Control]:::tactic
    D -->|T1486| E[Impact]:::tactic
    classDef actor fill:#1a0e2e,stroke:#a78bfa,color:#c9d8e8,font-weight:bold
    classDef tactic fill:#0d2038,stroke:#4da6ff,color:#c9d8e8

items.title: Vibe Hacking: Two AI-Augmented Campaigns Target Government and Financial Sectors in Latin America
items.description: Two distinct threat campaigns, SHADOW-AETHER-040 and SHADOW-AETHER-064, have been identified targeting government entities and financial organizations across Latin America using agentic artificial intelligence to conduct cyber intrusions. SHADOW-AETHER-040, a Spanish-speaking group, compromised six government entities in Mexico between December 2025 and January 2026, while SHADOW-AETHER-064, opera
items.ai_summary: The SHADOW-AETHER-040 and SHADOW-AETHER-064 campaigns, targeting government and financial sectors in Latin America, utilize AI-augmented techniques for cyber intrusions. These campaigns, active between December 2025 and January 2026, compromised six government entities in Mexico, with SHADOW-AETHER-040 being a Spanish-speaking group. The real-world impact includes potential data breaches and unauthorized access to sensitive information, affecting government entities and financial organizations. To remediate, defenders should implement robust network monitoring, utilize AI-powered intrusion detection systems, and ensure all software and systems are up-to-date, specifically focusing on secure configuration and patch management for operating systems and applications. Additionally, conducting regular security audits and providing cybersecurity awareness training to employees can help mitigate such threats.

graph LR

    B -->|T1059.001| C[Execution]:::tactic
    C -->|T1041| D[Command and Control]:::tactic

    classDef tactic fill:#0d2038,stroke:#4da6ff,color:#c9d8e8

items.title: Sniper's Nest: From Brand Impersonation to Browser Hijacking and CPA Fraud
items.description: An investigation into phishing activity targeting users across the Middle East and North Africa uncovered SniperDz, a centralized Push-Notification-as-a-Service and Phishing-as-a-Service platform. The operation uses fraudulent Facebook accounts impersonating politicians, public figures, and trusted organizations to promote fake offers including free mobile internet packages and financial compensat
items.ai_summary: The SniperDz operation utilizes phishing tactics, specifically T1566, to target users in the Middle East and North Africa, impersonating trusted entities such as politicians and public figures to promote fake offers. This campaign leverages fraudulent Facebook accounts to distribute phishing links, which when clicked, can lead to browser hijacking and CPA fraud. The threat actors behind SniperDz manipulate access tokens, as indicated by T1134, to gain unauthorized access to user accounts. To mitigate this threat, defenders should implement robust email and social media filters to detect and block phishing attempts, and educate users on how to identify and report suspicious activity. Additionally, users should be cautious when clicking on links from unfamiliar sources and verify the authenticity of offers before providing personal information.

graph LR
    A([Threat Actor]):::actor -->|T1566| B[Initial Access]:::tactic
    B -->|T1134| C[Privilege Escalation]:::tactic
    C -->|T1059.001| D[Execution]:::tactic
    D -->|T1204| E[Impact]:::tactic
    classDef actor fill:#1a0e2e,stroke:#a78bfa,color:#c9d8e8,font-weight:bold
    classDef tactic fill:#0d2038,stroke:#4da6ff,color:#c9d8e8

items.title: From external espionage to domestic targeting
items.description: Analysis of OceanLotus activities from 2024-2026 reveals a strategic shift toward domestic espionage within Vietnam. The Vietnam-aligned APT group conducted two distinct campaigns using the SPECTRALVIPER backdoor: a supply-chain attack compromising FireAnt Metakit stock trading platform from October 2025 to March 2026, and a prolonged intrusion into a Vietnamese infrastructure and transport constr
items.ai_summary: The OceanLotus APT group has shifted its focus towards domestic espionage within Vietnam, conducting two distinct campaigns using the SPECTRALVIPER backdoor. The first campaign involved a supply-chain attack on the FireAnt Metakit stock trading platform from October 2025 to March 2026, compromising the platform and potentially allowing the group to gain access to sensitive financial information. The second campaign involved a prolonged intrusion into a Vietnamese infrastructure and transport construction company, highlighting the group's ability to adapt and expand its targeting. To remediate these threats, defenders should implement robust network monitoring and detection capabilities, focusing on unusual patterns of remote access software usage, and ensure that all software and systems are up-to-date with the latest security patches. Additionally, organizations should conduct regular security audits and risk assessments to identify potential vulnerabilities and weaknesses in their systems and networks.
items.workflow_graph:
graph LR
    A([Threat Actor]):::actor -->|T1219| B[Remote Access]:::tactic
    B -->|T1059.001| C[Execution]:::tactic
    C -->|T1041| D[Command and Control]:::tactic
    D -->|T1486| E[Impact]:::tactic
    classDef actor fill:#1a0e2e,stroke:#a78bfa,color:#c9d8e8,font-weight:bold
    classDef tactic fill:#0d2038,stroke:#4da6ff,color:#c9d8e8

items.title: Targeted espionage against Cambodian government entities
items.description: Acronis Threat Research Unit identified two espionage campaigns targeting Cambodian government entities in defense and public works sectors, attributed to a cluster tracked as Khmer Shadow. Both campaigns delivered a custom C++ loader named NIGHTFORGE through government-themed lures in self-extracting archives. NIGHTFORGE employs sophisticated evasion techniques including NTDLL unhooking and Hell'
items.ai_summary: The NIGHTFORGE C++ loader is delivered through self-extracting archives with government-themed lures, targeting Cambodian government entities in the defense and public works sectors. This custom loader employs sophisticated evasion techniques, including NTDLL unhooking and Hell's Gate evasion, to avoid detection. If exploited, NIGHTFORGE can lead to data encryption and impairment of defenses, resulting in significant real-world impact, such as disruption of critical government services and potential data breaches. To remediate, defenders should implement robust email filtering to block suspicious attachments, ensure all systems are up-to-date with the latest security patches, and configure endpoint detection and response tools to monitor for unusual activity. Additionally, network traffic should be monitored for signs of command and control communication, and incident response plans should be in place to quickly respond to potential breaches.
items.workflow_graph:
graph LR
    A([Threat Actor]):::actor -->|T1204| B[User Execution]:::tactic
    B -->|T1106| C[Execution]:::tactic
    C -->|T1562| D[Impair Defenses]:::tactic
    D -->|T1486| E[Data Encrypted for Impact]:::tactic
    classDef actor fill:#1a0e2e,stroke:#a78bfa,color:#c9d8e8,font-weight:bold
    classDef tactic fill:#0d2038,stroke:#4da6ff,color:#c9d8e8

items.title: LBIOC-20260071 - The Gentlemens Leak
items.description: The Gentlemen is an active ransomware and extortion operation that emerged publicly in the second half of 2025, rapidly escalating into a high-volume threat actor. The group appears to be a continuation or reorganization of prior ransomware affiliate activity, with reported connections to the Qilin ecosystem and the Russian-speaking actor 'hastalamuerte.' This growth likely reflects existing ranso
items.ai_summary: The Gentlemen ransomware operation, also known as LBIOC-20260071, has been identified as a high-volume threat actor that emerged in the second half of 2025. This group is believed to be a continuation or reorganization of prior ransomware affiliate activity, with connections to the Qilin ecosystem and the Russian-speaking actor 'hastalamuerte.' The threat actor utilizes tactics such as stealing or forging authentication certificates (T1649) and encrypting data for impact (T1486), resulting in significant disruptions to affected organizations. To remediate this threat, defenders should prioritize implementing robust authentication mechanisms, such as multi-factor authentication, and ensuring that all systems and software are up-to-date with the latest security patches. Additionally, organizations should consider conducting regular security audits and implementing a robust backup and disaster recovery plan to minimize the impact of a potential ransomware attack.
items.workflow_graph:
graph LR
    A([Threat Actor]):::actor -->|T1649| B[Steal or Forge Authentication Certificates]:::tactic
    B -->|T1486| C[Data Encrypted for Impact]:::tactic
    C -->|T1070| D[Indicator Removal]:::tactic
    D -->|T1482| E[Data Encrypted for Impact]:::tactic
    classDef actor fill:#1a0e2e,stroke:#a78bfa,color:#c9d8e8,font-weight:bold
    classDef tactic fill:#0d2038,stroke:#4da6ff,color:#c9d8e8

items.title: Python Backdoor Threat Analysis Following an AI Deepfake Impersonation Campaign
items.description: A sophisticated campaign linked to APT37 delivers Python-based backdoors through spear-phishing emails containing malicious LNK files disguised as legitimate documents. Attackers use themes including airline e-tickets, North Korea research invitations, and impersonation of defense and police officials to induce execution. The LNK files employ environment variable-based obfuscation techniques to do
items.ai_summary: The Python backdoor threat is delivered through spear-phishing emails containing malicious LNK files disguised as legitimate documents, leveraging themes such as airline e-tickets and impersonation of defense officials to induce execution. The LNK files utilize environment variable-based obfuscation techniques to evade detection, and the backdoor allows attackers to manipulate access tokens and masquerade as legitimate users. If exploited, this threat could lead to unauthorized access to sensitive information and systems, with potential real-world impact including data breaches and disruption of critical infrastructure. To remediate, defenders should implement robust email filtering and employee education on phishing attacks, and ensure that all systems are configured to restrict the execution of LNK files from untrusted sources. Additionally, defenders should monitor for suspicious activity related to access token manipulation and remote access software, and update their security controls to detect and block the specific TTPs used in this campaign, including T1566, T1134, T1027, T1036, and T1219.
items.workflow_graph:
graph LR
    A([Threat Actor]):::actor -->|T1566| B[Initial Access]:::tactic
    B -->|T1027| C[Execution]:::tactic
    C -->|T1134| D[Persistence]:::tactic
    D -->|T1036| E[Defense Evasion]:::tactic
    classDef actor fill:#1a0e2e,stroke:#a78bfa,color:#c9d8e8,font-weight:bold
    classDef tactic fill:#0d2038,stroke:#4da6ff,color:#c9d8e8

items.title: ClickFix Evolves with PySoxy Proxying
items.description: A sophisticated ClickFix campaign was observed in April 2026 deploying PySoxy, a decade-old open-source Python SOCKS5 proxy tool, to establish encrypted proxy access on compromised hosts. The attack chain begins with social engineering that tricks users into executing obfuscated PowerShell commands, which then establishes scheduled task persistence and deploys an in-memory PowerShell-based command
items.ai_summary: The ClickFix campaign utilizes social engineering tactics to trick users into executing obfuscated PowerShell commands, which establish scheduled task persistence and deploy an in-memory PowerShell-based command. This attack chain exploits public-facing applications, as identified by TTP T1190, and utilizes PowerShell for execution, aligning with T1059.001. The real-world impact of this exploit includes the potential for unauthorized access to sensitive data and systems, with affected parties likely including organizations with vulnerable public-facing applications. To remediate this threat, defenders should implement robust input validation and sanitization for all public-facing applications, and ensure that all systems are configured to restrict the execution of obfuscated PowerShell commands. Additionally, defenders should monitor for suspicious scheduled tasks and command-line activity, and consider implementing a security orchestration, automation, and response (SOAR) solution to streamline incident response.
items.workflow_graph:
graph LR
    A([Threat Actor]):::actor -->|T1204| B[Initial Access]:::tactic
    B -->|T1059.001| C[Execution]:::tactic
    C -->|T1053| D[Persistence]:::tactic
    D -->|T1190| E[Impact]:::tactic
    classDef actor fill:#1a0e2e,stroke:#a78bfa,color:#c9d8e8,font-weight:bold
    classDef tactic fill:#0d2038,stroke:#4da6ff,color:#c9d8e8

items.title: Ongoing exploitation of Cisco Catalyst SD-WAN vulnerabilities
items.description: Cisco Talos tracks active exploitation of CVE-2026-20182, an authentication bypass vulnerability in Cisco Catalyst SD-WAN Controller and Manager, allowing remote attackers to obtain administrative privileges. The exploitation is attributed to UAT-8616, a sophisticated threat actor previously involved in similar attacks. Additionally, multiple threat clusters have been exploiting CVE-2026-20133, CV
items.ai_summary: The ongoing exploitation of Cisco Catalyst SD-WAN vulnerabilities, specifically CVE-2026-20182, allows remote attackers to bypass authentication and obtain administrative privileges. This vulnerability affects Cisco Catalyst SD-WAN Controller and Manager, and its exploitation is attributed to the sophisticated threat actor UAT-8616. If exploited, this vulnerability can lead to a full compromise of the SD-WAN infrastructure, allowing attackers to intercept and manipulate network traffic, as well as gain access to sensitive data. To remediate this vulnerability, defenders should immediately update their Cisco Catalyst SD-WAN Controller and Manager to the latest version, which includes the patched software, and ensure that all configurations are set to enforce strong authentication and authorization. Additionally, defenders should monitor their network traffic for suspicious activity and implement additional security controls, such as intrusion detection and prevention systems, to detect and prevent similar attacks.
items.workflow_graph:
graph LR
    A([Threat Actor]):::actor -->|T1190| B[Exploit Public-Facing Application]:::tactic
    B -->|T1566| C[Initial Access]:::tactic
    C -->|T1059.001| D[Execution]:::tactic
    D -->|T1486| E[Impact]:::tactic
    classDef actor fill:#1a0e2e,stroke:#a78bfa,color:#c9d8e8,font-weight:bold
    classDef tactic fill:#0d2038,stroke:#4da6ff,color:#c9d8e8

items.title: Gremlin Stealer's Evolved Tactics: Hiding in Plain Sight With Resource Files
items.description: This analysis examines new obfuscation techniques employed by Gremlin stealer malware to conceal malicious payloads within embedded resources. A variant protected by sophisticated commercial packing utility uses instruction virtualization, transforming code into custom bytecode executed by a private virtual machine. The malware siphons sensitive information including payment card details, browser
items.ai_summary: The Gremlin stealer malware has evolved its tactics to hide in plain sight by concealing malicious payloads within embedded resource files, utilizing sophisticated commercial packing utilities that employ instruction virtualization. This transformation of code into custom bytecode, executed by a private virtual machine, makes it challenging for defenders to detect. The malware targets sensitive information, including payment card details and browser data, and is categorized under incident response due to its potential for significant real-world impact, such as financial loss and identity theft. To remediate, defenders should implement robust file integrity monitoring, focusing on unusual patterns in resource files, and ensure up-to-date endpoint detection and response tools are in place, specifically looking for signs of virtual machine-based obfuscation. Additionally, network traffic monitoring should be enhanced to detect and block any suspicious communication that may indicate the exfiltration of sensitive data.
items.workflow_graph:
graph LR
    A([Threat Actor]):::actor -->|T1190| B[Exploit Public-Facing Application]:::tactic
    B -->|T1027| C[Obfuscated Files or Information]:::tactic
    C -->|T1055| D[Collection]:::tactic
    D -->|T1041| E[Command and Control]:::tactic
    classDef actor fill:#1a0e2e,stroke:#a78bfa,color:#c9d8e8,font-weight:bold
    classDef tactic fill:#0d2038,stroke:#4da6ff,color:#c9d8e8

items.title: CVE-2026-11347: The linqi application contains hardcoded cryptographic keys. Additionally, the a...
items.description: The linqi application contains hardcoded cryptographic keys. Additionally, the application uses a weak algorithm with a limited ASCII charset to dynamically generate Initialization Vectors (IVs) for AES/CBC encryption, making known-plaintext attacks feasible. An attacker with local access can leverage these vulnerabilities to decrypt sensitive obfuscated strings, including ConnectionString values
items.ai_summary: CVE-2026-11347 is a vulnerability in the linqi application that contains hardcoded cryptographic keys, allowing attackers with local access to decrypt sensitive information. The application also uses a weak algorithm to generate Initialization Vectors (IVs) for AES/CBC encryption, making it vulnerable to known-plaintext attacks. If exploited, an attacker can access ConnectionString values and other sensitive data, potentially leading to unauthorized access to databases or other systems. To remediate this vulnerability, users should update to a version of the linqi application that uses secure key management and encryption practices, such as using unique, randomly generated keys and secure protocols like AES/GCM. Additionally, users should ensure that all sensitive data is properly encrypted and access controls are in place to prevent unauthorized access.
items.workflow_graph:
graph LR
    A([Threat Actor]):::actor -->|T1027| B[Obfuscated Files or Information]:::tactic
    B -->|T1055| C[Uncovered Hidden Functionality]:::tactic
    C -->|T1212| D[Exploitation for Credential Access]:::tactic
    D -->|T1552| E[Uncovered Credentials]:::tactic
    classDef actor fill:#1a0e2e,stroke:#a78bfa,color:#c9d8e8,font-weight:bold
    classDef tactic fill:#0d2038,stroke:#4da6ff,color:#c9d8e8

items.title: CVE-2026-6274: Improper Authentication, Missing authentication for critical function, Weak Auth...
items.description:
Improper Authentication, Missing authentication for critical function, Weak Authentication vulnerability in DTS Electronics Industry and Trade Ltd. Co. Redline WR3200 allows Accessing Functionality Not Properly Constrained by ACLs.

This issue affects Redline WR3200: from 7.1.3 before 7.1.8.
items.ai_summary: CVE-2026-6274 is a critical vulnerability in DTS Electronics Industry and Trade Ltd. Co.'s Redline WR3200, specifically affecting versions prior to 7.1.8. This improper authentication vulnerability allows attackers to access functionality not properly constrained by ACLs, potentially leading to unauthorized access and control. If exploited, this could result in significant real-world impact, including data breaches and disruption of critical services, particularly affecting organizations relying on the Redline WR3200. To remediate, defenders should immediately update to version 7.1.8 or later and ensure that all configurations are properly set to enforce ACLs, specifically focusing on critical functions that were previously accessible without proper authentication. Additionally, monitoring for unusual access patterns and implementing additional security measures such as multi-factor authentication can help mitigate potential attacks.

graph LR

    B -->|T1055| C[Privilege Escalation]:::tactic

items.title: CVE-2026-49777: Improper Validation of Specified Quantity in Input vulnerability in ShapedPlugin...
items.description:
Improper Validation of Specified Quantity in Input vulnerability in ShapedPlugin, LLC Product Slider Pro for WooCommerce allows Malicious Software Implanted.

This issue affects Product Slider Pro for WooCommerce: from n/a before 3.5.4.
items.ai_summary: The CVE-2026-49777 vulnerability is an improper validation of specified quantity in input vulnerability in ShapedPlugin, LLC's Product Slider Pro for WooCommerce, allowing malicious software to be implanted. This issue affects versions of Product Slider Pro for WooCommerce prior to 3.5.4, and can be exploited by malicious actors through public-facing applications, as indicated by the detected TTP T1190. If exploited, this vulnerability can lead to the implantation of malicious software, potentially resulting in data breaches, ransomware attacks, or other malicious activities. To remediate this vulnerability, defenders should update Product Slider Pro for WooCommerce to version 3.5.4 or later, and ensure that all plugins and themes are up-to-date and configured correctly. Additionally, defenders should monitor their systems for suspicious activity and implement robust security measures, such as web application firewalls and intrusion detection systems, to prevent exploitation.

items.title: CVE-2026-11332: A flaw was found in ansible-core. The ansible-galaxy role install command proces...
items.description: A flaw was found in ansible-core. The ansible-galaxy role install command processes dependency specifications from a role's meta/requirements.yml file. Due to improper neutralization of argument delimiters, a malicious role author can inject arbitrary git configuration flags through the src field. This allows arbitrary code execution on the machine of a user who installs the role via ansible-galax
items.ai_summary: CVE-2026-11332 is a vulnerability in ansible-core that allows for arbitrary code execution on a user's machine when installing a malicious role via ansible-galaxy. The vulnerability arises from the improper neutralization of argument delimiters in the src field of a role's meta/requirements.yml file, enabling a malicious role author to inject arbitrary git configuration flags. This can lead to a range of malicious activities, including data breaches and lateral movement within a network. To remediate this vulnerability, users should update to ansible-core version 2.14.0 or later and ensure that only trusted roles are installed. Additionally, users can mitigate the risk by implementing strict role validation and verification processes, such as manually reviewing the meta/requirements.yml file for suspicious dependencies.
items.workflow_graph:

    A([Threat Actor]):::actor -->|T1072| B[Software Deployment Tools]:::tactic
    B -->|T1059| C[Command and Scripting Interpreter]:::tactic
    C -->|T1190| D[Trusted Relationship]:::tactic
    D -->|T1486| E[Impact]:::tactic
    classDef actor fill:#1a0e2e,stroke:#a78bfa,color:#c9d8e8,font-weight:bold
    classDef tactic fill:#0d2038,stroke:#4da6ff,color:#c9d8e8

items.title: CVE-2026-9088: A flaw was found in org.keycloak.services. An administrator with delegated acces...
items.description: A flaw was found in org.keycloak.services. An administrator with delegated access to read group memberships and users can bypass user profile permissions by accessing the group members endpoint. This allows the administrator to view user attributes that are explicitly configured to be denied, leading to information disclosure.
items.ai_summary: CVE-2026-9088 is a vulnerability in org.keycloak.services that allows an administrator with delegated access to bypass user profile permissions, leading to information disclosure. The attack vector involves accessing the group members endpoint to view user attributes that are explicitly configured to be denied. This vulnerability affects Keycloak administrators with delegated access to read group memberships and users, potentially impacting organizations that rely on Keycloak for identity and access management. To remediate this vulnerability, administrators should update to a patched version of Keycloak and review their delegation settings to ensure that administrators do not have excessive access to sensitive user attributes. Additionally, organizations should monitor their Keycloak logs for suspicious activity related to group membership and user attribute access.

items.description: HCL Digital Experience is affected by an OS command injection vulnerability in the Digital Asset Management API.  An attacker may execute arbitrary operating system commands, typically inheriting the privileges of the vulnerable application, which could possibly lead to a complete system takeover and data compromise.

items.title: FBI disrupts massive AI-powered phishing service using a million URLs
items.description: In a coordinated effort, the FBI, working with Google and Black Lotus Labs, has dismantled a massive Chinese phishing-as-a-service operation called Outsider Enterprise with thousands of phishing websites used to steal credit card data and passwords. [...]

items.title: Ex-school district employee jailed for hacks on former employer
items.description: A former IT employee at an Iowa school district was sentenced to 21 months in prison after conducting a prolonged cyberattack against the former employer that disrupted classroom operations, deleted accounts, and caused tens of thousands of dollars in damages. [...]

items.title: NPM 12 Will Change Script Execution Behavior to Prevent Supply Chain Attacks
items.description: By default, npm install will no longer execute scripts from dependencies, unless explicitly allowed. The post NPM 12 Will Change Script Execution Behavior to Prevent Supply Chain Attacks appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: Chinese hackers hijack auth flow, spy on isolated network for a decade
items.description: Chinese hackers took control of a target organization's authentication stack and maintained persistence for 10 years, with full visibility into the administrative activity. [...]

items.title: Critical Splunk Enterprise Flaw Lets Attackers Run Code Without Authentication
items.description: Splunk has released security updates to address a critical security flaw in Splunk Enterprise that could be exploited to conduct unauthenticated file operations and even remote code execution. The vulnerability, tracked as CVE-2026-20253, is rated 9.8 on the CVSS scoring system. "In Splunk Enterprise versions below 10.2.4 and 10.0.7, an unauthenticated user could create or truncate arbitrary

items.title: US Gov asks Anthropic to ban 'foreign national' access to Fable, Mythos
items.description: The US government has ordered Anthropic to block all foreign nationals from accessing Fable 5 and Mythos 5, forcing the company to suspend both models worldwide. Anthropic is complying but disputes the basis, calling the cited jailbreak narrow and the capability widely available elsewhere. [...]

items.title: Anthropic Says It Has Taken Its Latest AI Models Offline to Comply With New Export Controls
items.description: Anthropic takes Fable 5 and Mythos 5 offline to comply with a directive from the Trump administration to prevent use by foreign nationals. The post Anthropic Says It Has Taken Its Latest AI Models Offline to Comply With New Export Controls appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: U.S. Orders Anthropic to Suspend Fable 5 and Mythos 5 Access for Foreign Nationals
items.description: Anthropic said on Friday it will "abruptly disable" its most advanced artificial intelligence (AI) models, Claude Fable 5 and Mythos 5, for all users after the U.S. government ordered it to suspend access to the models for foreign nationals, whether inside or outside the U.S., citing national security concerns. The AI company said it received an order at 5:21 p.m. ET, instructing it to suspend

items.title: ShinyHunters Uses Oracle Zero-Day to Rampage Higher Ed
items.description: A major bug in Oracle's ERP software disproportionately affected American universities, and hackers have capitalized by stealing gobs of data.

items.title: Maine disables data breach notification portal after fake disclosures
items.description: Maine has taken its public data breach reporting portal offline after fraudulent breach disclosures were published on the state's website, prompting a review of procedures to prevent abuse in the future. [...]

items.title: Over 400 Arch Linux AUR Packages Hijacked to Deploy Infostealer and eBPF Rootkit
items.description: Attackers took over more than 400 packages in the Arch User Repository (AUR) this week and rewrote their build scripts to install a credential stealer on any machine that built them. The malware is a Rust binary built to harvest developer secrets. When it lands with root, it can also load an eBPF rootkit to hide itself. The AUR is Arch Linux's community package collection, and it is separate

items.title: Google Sues Chinese Smishing Network Accused of Using Gemini AI in Phishing
items.description: Google on Friday said it's pursuing legal action against a Chinese cybercrime network, accusing it of using its Gemini artificial intelligence (AI) agent to send phishing text messages targeting Americans. The network is said to be behind the development and management of a phishing-as-a-service (PhaaS) software kit called Outsider, per the tech giant. "The operation weaponized Gemini to help

items.title: phpBB forum fixes auth bypass bug lurking for a decade
items.description: A 10-year-old authentication bypass vulnerability discovered in the phpBB forum software allows an attacker to log in as any user, including administrators. [...]

items.title: China-Linked Hackers Backdoored Linux Login Software to Hide for Nearly a Decade
items.description: Instead of hiding on the laptops and servers defenders watch most closely, a China-nexus group spent close to a decade hidden inside the Linux login system itself. Sygnia, which tracks the group as Velvet Ant, says it backdoored the PAM and OpenSSH components that decide who is allowed to sign in, planting its access where ordinary cleanup could not reach it. The network it targeted had no

items.title: Ukrainian national pleads guilty to role in Conti ransomware operation
items.description: A Ukrainian national extradited from Ireland to the United States last year has pleaded guilty to conspiracy charges tied to the Conti ransomware operation. [...]

items.title: Over 400 Arch Linux packages compromised to push rootkit, infostealer
items.description: More than 400 packages in the Arch User Repository (AUR) are distributing a Linux rootkit and infostealer malware targeting credentials and access tokens. [...]

items.title: In Other News: Google Security Layoffs, AudiA6 Takedown, $400 Million Coupang Fine
items.description: Other noteworthy stories that might have slipped under the radar: ICS device exposure remains flat as attack surface widens, Microsoft issues incident response playbook for AI, IBM and AT&T accused of hack cover-ups. The post In Other News: Google Security Layoffs, AudiA6 Takedown, $400 Million Coupang Fine appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: Early Warning Signs of Supply-Chain Attacks Live in the Dark Web
items.description: GitHub access sales, leaked repositories, and stolen API keys can all become supply-chain attack footholds. Flare explores how underground forums expose early signals tied to software supply-chain risk. [...]

items.title: Claude Fable 5 Doesn't Change the Mythos Security Story
items.description: Stay cool: Mythos 5 is an upgrade over Mythos Preview while Fable 5 is Mythos "made safe for general use," Anthropic explained.

items.title: Industry Reactions to Claude Fable 5: Feedback Friday
items.description: Industry professionals comment on various aspects of Fable 5, including dual-use capabilities, safeguards, and tiered access. The post Industry Reactions to Claude Fable 5: Feedback Friday appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: ISC Stormcast For Friday, June 12th, 2026 https://isc.sans.edu/podcastdetail/9970, (Fri, Jun 12th)
items.description: (c) SANS Internet Storm Center. https://isc.sans.edu Creative Commons Attribution-Noncommercial 3.0 United States License.

items.title: Agentjacking Attack Tricks AI Coding Agents Into Running Malicious Code
items.description: Cybersecurity researchers have described what they say is a new class of attack that can trick artificial intelligence (AI) coding agents into running arbitrary code on developer machines. Called Agentjacking by Tenet Security, the attack can be triggered by means of a fake error report crafted using Sentry, an open-source error-tracking and performance-monitoring platform. "The attack

items.title: Microsoft fixes Windows update failures linked to WUSA installer
items.description: Microsoft has fixed a known issue that caused Windows updates released since May 2025 to fail when installed via the Windows Update Standalone Installer (WUSA) from a network share. [...]

items.title: Iranian Cyber Group Handala Claims Cal Water Hack
items.description: The hackers published 5GB of data, including customer personal information and credentials for the RTKBase platform. The post Iranian Cyber Group Handala Claims Cal Water Hack appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: Rethinking MDR as Attackers and Defenders Embrace AI
items.description: For most of the past decade, managed detection and response was the answer to a real problem. Security teams couldn't staff around the clock, couldn't hire enough analysts, and needed someone else to handle the alert queue. MDR stepped in. It worked well enough. Until now. The threat landscape has changed faster than the MDR model can adapt. Attackers are using AI to move faster, generate more

items.title: LangGraph Flaw Chain Exposes Self-Hosted AI Agents to Remote Code Execution
items.description: Cybersecurity researchers have disclosed details of three now-patched security flaws impacting LangGraph, including a critical vulnerability chain that could result in remote code execution. LangGraph is an open-source framework created by LangChain to build complex, stateful, and multi-agent artificial intelligence (AI) agentic applications. "An SQL injection in LangGraph's function could

items.title: Ivanti Sentry Exploitation Attempts Hitting Honeypots
items.description: The critical-severity OS command injection vulnerability allows attackers to execute arbitrary code with root privileges. The post Ivanti Sentry Exploitation Attempts Hitting Honeypots appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: Chrome 149 Update Patches 28 Vulnerabilities
items.description: The browser refresh resolved critical and high-severity security defects, including a dozen use-after-free bugs. The post Chrome 149 Update Patches 28 Vulnerabilities appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: INTERPOL Operation Takes Down Sniper Dz Phishing Platform, Arrests Administrator
items.description: An INTERPOL-led operation last month resulted in the disruption of Sniper Dz, a decade-long phishing-as-a-service (PhaaS) platform, Group-IB said Thursday. The effort, codenamed Operation Ramz, took place between October 2025 and February 2026, and saw authorities from 13 countries in the Middle East and North Africa (MENA) region making 201 arrests. Included among them was Guedz, the primary

items.title: Anthropic Disputes Fable 5 AI Jailbreak
items.description: An AI hacker claims to have achieved a prompt-based jailbreak shortly after Fable 5’s launch, but Anthropic says it’s not a real jailbreak. The post Anthropic Disputes Fable 5 AI Jailbreak appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: Google Confirms Exploitation of Oracle PeopleSoft Zero-Day by ShinyHunters
items.description: Oracle has mitigated CVE-2026-35273, but it has not publicly confirmed the vulnerability’s in-the-wild exploitation. The post Google Confirms Exploitation of Oracle PeopleSoft Zero-Day by ShinyHunters appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: Europol Disrupts AudiA6 Crypto Laundering Service Used by Ransomware Gangs
items.description: Authorities in Europe have disrupted AudiA6, a cryptocurrency laundering service used by ransomware gangs and cybercriminal networks. Europol, in a statement issued Thursday, said the dismantling of AudiA6 cut off a "key financial pipeline used to wash hundreds of millions in illicit profits." The service is estimated to have been used to launder more than €336 million (~$389 million) since the

items.title: Phishing Attack Volume Down 20%, But Risk Still Rising
items.description: Hackers are valuing quality over quantity, using AI to upgrade their phishing attacks rather than multiply them.

items.title: Max-Severity Ivanti Flaw Exploited 24 Hours After Disclosure
items.description: Initial methods suggest attackers had likely mapped out Ivanti's asset landscape upfront and acted quickly once the exploit became public.

items.title: Segmentation Works for OT If Operators Are Paying Attention
items.description: Even the best segmentation strategy will fall apart without constant oversight and disciplined operations.

items.title: Oracle Addresses PeopleSoft Vulnerability Amid Reports of Zero-Day Attacks
items.description: Oracle has released mitigations for CVE-2026-35273, but it has not said whether it’s a zero-day exploited in ShinyHunters attacks. The post Oracle Addresses PeopleSoft Vulnerability Amid Reports of Zero-Day Attacks appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: ISC Stormcast For Thursday, June 11th, 2026 https://isc.sans.edu/podcastdetail/9968, (Thu, Jun 11th)
items.description: (c) SANS Internet Storm Center. https://isc.sans.edu Creative Commons Attribution-Noncommercial 3.0 United States License.

items.title: Chinese, N. Korean Threat Groups Build on Asia-Pacific Success
items.description: North Korea's gross domestic product (GDP) has grown, in part because of the nation's state-sponsored cybercrime groups, which target financial firms and other businesses.

items.title: CISA Rewrites Federal Patching Requirements for AI Threat Era
items.description: The new directive gives federal agencies three days to fix the most dangerous flaws, while less severe issues can be deferred.

items.title: Bug Bounty Research Triggers ServiceNow Security Alert
items.description: Security research inadvertently led organizations to believe they were being breached through their ServiceNow instances.

items.title: AI Risk Worries Insurers & Businesses Alike
items.description: As companies adopt AI, many insurance firms are explicitly excluding AI risks, while others are forging ahead to create the right framework. What risks can firms reasonably manage?

items.title: Nightmare-Eclipse Drops Yet Another Microsoft Exploit, RoguePlanet
items.description: The disgruntled researcher released yet another PoC for a Windows Defender bug that allows for system takeover, showing no signs of abandoning their ongoing feud with Microsoft.

items.title: Who Runs the Ransomware Group ‘The Gentlemen?’
items.description: A cybercrime group known as The Gentlemen has emerged as the second most active ransomware gang by victim count, rapidly attracting a talented pool of hackers through an aggressive recruitment strategy that promises affiliates 90 percent of any ransom paid by victims. This post examines clues pointing to a real life identity for the administrator of The Gentlemen ransomware group.

items.title: CISA Issues New Directive Improving How Federal Agencies Prioritize the Mitigation of Cyber Vulnerabilities
How has use of framing protection security headers changed in the past 3 years?, (Wed, Jun 10th)
items.description: Back in 2023, I wrote a diary[1] discussing how commonly X-Frame-Options and CSP headers containing the frame-ancestors directive were used on 1 million most popular domains on the internet (based on the Tranco list[2]), and how they were set. Given that three years have passed since then, I thought it might be interesting to repeat the analysis and see what â€“ if anything â€“ has changed in the meantime.

items.title: ISC Stormcast For Wednesday, June 10th, 2026 https://isc.sans.edu/podcastdetail/9966, (Wed, Jun 10th)
items.description: (c) SANS Internet Storm Center. https://isc.sans.edu Creative Commons Attribution-Noncommercial 3.0 United States License.

items.title: A Record-Breaking Patch Tuesday for June 2026
items.description: Microsoft today released software updates to plug nearly 200 security holes across its Windows operating systems and supported software, a record number of fixes for the company's monthly Patch Tuesday cycle. Nearly three dozen of those bugs earned Microsoft's most dire "critical" rating, and exploit code for at least three of the weaknesses is now publicly available.

items.title: Microsoft June 2026 Patch Tuesday, (Tue, Jun 9th)
items.description: Microsoft today released patches for 204 vulnerabilities. 38 of these vulnerabilities are considered critical, and three have been disclosed before today. Six of the vulnerabilities affect Microsoft cloud solutions and do not require any user action. In addition, Microsoft incorporated 360 different vulnerabilities affecting Chromium into its Edge browser.

items.title: CISA Announces Winners of the 2026 President’s Cup Cybersecurity Competition
ISC Stormcast For Tuesday, June 9th, 2026 https://isc.sans.edu/podcastdetail/9964, (Tue, Jun 9th)
items.description: (c) SANS Internet Storm Center. https://isc.sans.edu Creative Commons Attribution-Noncommercial 3.0 United States License.

items.title: TeamPCP Supply Chain Campaign: Activity Through 2026-06-07, (Mon, Jun 8th)
items.description: This diary continues the Internet Storm Center&&#x23;x26;&#x23;39;s tracking of the TeamPCP supply chain campaign, first documented in the SANS white paper When the Security Scanner Became the Weapon and most recently in the handler diary Activity Through 2026-05-24. Since that update, the story moved into two new places: the United States government, which formally caught up to the campaign, and the wider population of attackers now wielding the Mini Shai-Hulud framework that TeamPCP open-sourced last month.

items.title: ISC Stormcast For Monday, June 8th, 2026 https://isc.sans.edu/podcastdetail/9962, (Mon, Jun 8th)
items.description: (c) SANS Internet Storm Center. https://isc.sans.edu Creative Commons Attribution-Noncommercial 3.0 United States License.

items.title: The Evil MSI Background is Back!, (Fri, Jun 5th)
items.description: A few months ago, I wrote a diary about a payload that was embedded into a JPEG picture. It was a MSI-branded background[1]. Yesterday, I spotted another one! It seems that the technic is getting more and more popular. This time, it started with a mail containing a WeTransfer link.

items.title: CISA Urges Stronger Security for Automatic Tank Gauge Systems
Hackers Used Meta’s AI Support Bot to Seize Instagram Accounts
items.description: The Instagram accounts for the Obama White House and the Chief Master Sergeant of the U.S. Space Force were briefly defaced with pro-Iranian images and messages over the weekend, after instructions began circulating on Telegram showing how to trick Meta's "AI support assistant" bot into resetting account passwords.

items.title: CISA Announces Revised Town Hall Schedule to Engage with Stakeholders on Cyber Incident Reporting for Critical Infrastructure
Netherlands Seizes 800 Servers, Arrests 2 for Aiding Cyberattacks
items.description: Authorities in the Netherlands have arrested the co-owners of two related Internet hosting companies for operating IT infrastructure used by Russia to carry out cyberattacks, influence operations and disinformation campaigns inside the European Union. The two men were the focus of a 2025 KrebsOnSecurity story about how their hosting companies had assumed control over the technical infrastructure of Stark Industries Solutions, an Internet service provider sanctioned last year by the EU as a frequent staging ground for cyber mischief from Russia's intelligence agencies.

items.title: Lawmakers Demand Answers as CISA Tries to Contain Data Leak
items.description: Lawmakers in both houses of Congress are demanding answers from the U.S. Cybersecurity & Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) after KrebsOnSecurity reported this week that a CISA contractor intentionally published AWS GovCloud keys and a vast trove of other agency secrets on a public GitHub account. The inquiry comes as CISA is still struggling to contain the breach and invalidate the leaked credentials.

items.title: Alleged Kimwolf Botmaster ‘Dort’ Arrested, Charged in U.S. and Canada
items.description: Canadian authorities on Wednesday arrested a 23-year-old Ottawa man on suspicion of building and operating Kimwolf, a fast spreading Internet-of-Things botnet that enslaved millions of devices for use in a series of massive distributed denial-of-service (DDoS) attacks over the past six months. KrebsOnSecurity publicly named the suspect in February 2026 after the accused launched a volley of DDoS, doxing and swatting campaigns against this author and a security researcher. He now faces criminal hacking charges in both Canada and the United States.

items.title: CISA Enhances Known Exploited Vulnerabilities Catalog to Include New Nomination Form
CISA Admin Leaked AWS GovCloud Keys on Github
items.description: Until this past weekend, a contractor for the Cybersecurity & Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) maintained a public GitHub repository that exposed credentials to several highly privileged AWS GovCloud accounts and a large number of internal CISA systems. Security experts said the public archive included files detailing how CISA builds, tests and deploys software internally, and that it represents one of the most egregious government data leaks in recent history.

items.title: Patch Tuesday, May 2026 Edition
items.description: Artificial intelligence platforms may be just as susceptible to social engineering as human beings, but they are proving remarkably good at finding security vulnerabilities in human-made computer code. That reality is on full display this month with some of the more widely-used software makers -- including Apple, Google, Microsoft, Mozilla and Oracle -- fixing near record volumes of security bugs, and/or quickening the tempo of their patch releases.

items.title: Canvas Breach Disrupts Schools & Colleges Nationwide
items.description: An ongoing data extortion attack targeting the widely-used education technology platform Canvas disrupted classes and coursework at school districts and universities across the United States today, after a cybercrime group defaced the service's login page with a ransom demand that threatened to leak data from 275 million students and faculty across nearly 9,000 educational institutions.

items.title: CISA Unveils New Initiative to Fortify America’s Critical Infrastructure
CISA, US and International Partners Release Guide to Secure Adoption of Agentic AI
Anti-DDoS Firm Heaped Attacks on Brazilian ISPs
items.description: A Brazilian tech firm that specializes in protecting networks from distributed denial-of-service (DDoS) attacks has been enabling a botnet responsible for an extended campaign of massive DDoS attacks against other network operators in Brazil, KrebsOnSecurity has learned. The firm's chief executive says the malicious activity resulted from a security breach and was likely the work of a competitor trying to tarnish his company's public image.

items.title: CISA and U.S. Government Partners Unveil Guide to Accelerate Zero Trust Adoption in Operational Technology
CISA, National Cyber Security Centre (NCSC) UK, and Global Partners Issue Advisory on Chinese Government-Linked Covert Cyber Networks
CISA Warns of FIRESTARTER Malware Targeting Cisco ASA including Firepower and Secure Firewall Products
Student Loan Breach Exposes 2.5M Records
items.description: 2.5 million people were affected, in a breach that could spell more trouble down the line.

items.title: Watering Hole Attacks Push ScanBox Keylogger
items.description: Researchers uncover a watering hole attack likely carried out by APT TA423, which attempts to plant the ScanBox JavaScript-based reconnaissance tool.

items.title: Tentacles of ‘0ktapus’ Threat Group Victimize 130 Firms
items.description: Over 130 companies tangled in sprawling phishing campaign that spoofed a multi-factor authentication system.

items.title: Ransomware Attacks are on the Rise
items.description: Lockbit is by far this summer’s most prolific ransomware group, trailed by two offshoots of the Conti group.

items.title: Cybercriminals Are Selling Access to Chinese Surveillance Cameras
items.description: Tens of thousands of cameras have failed to patch a critical, 11-month-old CVE, leaving thousands of organizations exposed.

items.title: Twitter Whistleblower Complaint: The TL;DR Version
items.description: Twitter is blasted for security and privacy lapses by the company’s former head of security who alleges the social media giant’s actions amount to a national security risk.

items.title: Firewall Bug Under Active Attack Triggers CISA Warning
items.description: CISA is warning that Palo Alto Networks’ PAN-OS is under active attack and needs to be patched ASAP.

items.title: Fake Reservation Links Prey on Weary Travelers
items.description: Fake travel reservations are exacting more pain from the travel weary, already dealing with the misery of canceled flights and overbooked hotels.

items.title: iPhone Users Urged to Update to Patch 2 Zero-Days
items.description: Separate fixes to macOS and iOS patch respective flaws in the kernel and WebKit that can allow threat actors to take over devices and are under attack.

items.title: Google Patches Chrome’s Fifth Zero-Day of the Year
items.description: An insufficient validation input flaw, one of 11 patched in an update this week, could allow for arbitrary code execution and is under active attack.
